title: Formulas Using Columns Summaries
excerpt: Is there anyway to use a formula to calculate a value from several column summaries? For example, if I have 3 column summaries (Column A, Column B, Column C), I’d like a formula that could provide a value for formula = (…

posts.author: mfildes (Matthew Fildes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there anyway to use a formula to calculate a value from several column summaries? For example, if I have 3 column summaries (Column A, Column B, Column C), I’d like a formula that could provide a value for formula = (Column A Summary-Column B Summary)/Column C Summary. Is this currently possible in base monday.com product, or with an add-on app?

In addition, I’d like the values in the column summaries to change based on the information being filtered and have the formula adjust automatically.

posts.author: mfildes (Matthew Fildes)
posts.cookedHtml: I created an example board to show the issue:

posts.author: heimesk (Kent)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi… Did you find a free solution to this? I have the same request.
Thanks

posts.author: allan735 (allan berner)
posts.cookedHtml: Mondaycom currently doesn’t directly support formulas using column summaries. Workarounds include creating helper columns with automations or using third-party apps, but these have limitations. Consider using external tools like Google Sheets for complex calculations and importing the results back into Mondaycom.

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml: YEs, (if Im understanding correctly), you would need to do a ‘add’ formula in a separate column and that separate formula column would then have a summarizing at the bottom in which you can showcase into a ‘number’ widget in a dash board.
